description: C4 /healthz returns 200 when the service is up
C4 boots with all required env present
C4 fails fast with a clear error when the private-key file is missing
/sign produces a JWT that verifies with packages/licensing verifyLicense (v1-test keypair)
Signed token header carries the expected kid
Signed claims match inputs (tier, seats, cust, sub)
exp is ~31 days out for a connected term
exp is 365 days out for an air-gap term
Ported signer output parity with ee/tools/alga-license/sign.mjs (same shape/claims)
Entitlement row is created/updated for a subscription on /sign
Claim code persisted with TTL and consumed flag
Appliance registration stores a hashed credential, never plaintext
Entitlement-store migrations create the expected schema
/sign returns a JWT for valid params
/sign rejects a caller without the service credential (401)
/sign rejects an invalid tier
/sign rejects non-numeric/negative seats
/sign rejects essentials/solo tiers (never sold)
/claim-codes mints a unique, single-use code
/claim-codes requires the service credential
/register with a valid code returns credential + first_jwt + check_in_url
/register rejects an unknown code
/register rejects an expired code
/register rejects an already-consumed code
Second use of a consumed code fails (single-use)
Concurrent /register on the same code yields exactly one success
/register binds the appliance to the subscription
Minting a fresh claim code revokes the prior appliance credential (rebind)
/check-in with a valid credential returns the current JWT
/check-in rejects a bad/unknown credential
/check-in returns 'no change' when the appliance holds the current version
/check-in returns a fresh JWT after a re-sign (renewal)
/check-in stops serving after /revoke (soft revocation)
/check-in updates last_checkin_at
Check-in carries no telemetry fields (thin payload)
/revoke marks a subscription entitlement inactive
Structured error returned for an invalid claim code
Structured error returned for a revoked appliance at check-in
Structured error returned for bad sign params
License-order service-request type is registered/discoverable
License-order form renders tier/seats/term fields
Form validation rejects a missing tier
Form validation rejects seats < 1
Form validation rejects an invalid term
A valid submission is stored as a service_request_submission with the order payload
The license-order provider is registered in the enterprise registry
A license-order request appears in the portal service-request list/status
The portal entry point starts a license-order request
SKU/price config resolves the correct price by tier x term
A Stripe Checkout Session is created from a license-order submission
Session metadata contains submission_id, tier, seats, term, transport, client_id
Connected term maps to subscription mode
Air-gap term maps to an annual/one-time term
Operator is redirected to the hosted Checkout URL
Success URL returns to the portal
Cancel URL returns to the portal
Webhook handles checkout.session.completed for a license order
Webhook branches non-license-order events; SaaS-seat handling unaffected
Duplicate webhook event is deduped via payment_webhook_events (no double fire)
Webhook correlates back to the submission via metadata
Webhook fires the issuance workflow
Expired/canceled checkout produces no issuance
Webhook signature verification is enforced
Replay of the same Stripe event id is deduped
Issuance workflow is triggered on a completed payment
Workflow id equals license-issue:{payment_intent_id}
Duplicate payment event results in a single workflow run
Activity derives ~31d exp for connected
Activity derives 365d exp for air-gap
Activity calls C4 /sign and receives a JWT
A failed /sign is retried by Temporal
Retry after partial failure does not double-issue (one contract/doc/code)
client_contracts assignment is upserted with tier/term/start/end
A license contract_line is upserted with tier/seats
renewal_mode is set on the contract for air-gap annual
The signed JWT is stored as a client document (entity_type='client')
The document is associated to the contract (entity_type='contract')
Connected issuance mints a claim code via /claim-codes
Air-gap issuance does NOT mint a claim code
Connected issuance email contains the claim code
Air-gap issuance email contains the JWT
Issuance email contains a portal link
Renewal event extends contract end_date and re-signs (connected)
Renewal event re-delivers an annual JWT (air-gap)
Cancellation calls C4 /revoke and marks the contract terminated
Air-gap pre-expiry nudge re-delivers a fresh annual JWT respecting notice_period_days
Document content is the JWT and is retrievable + valid
Delivery failure (email) does not lose the contract/doc; retry delivers
createUser enforces license seats in self-host mode at the EE tier
essentials is unmetered (no seat limit)
pro/premium enforce the license seats at user creation
SaaS path (licensed_user_count) is unchanged when no license_state row exists
Reaching the appliance seat limit returns a clear error
Seat count is based on active internal users
Existing SaaS seat-purchase flow (LicensePurchaseForm / licence-count) still works (regression)
Migration adds appliance_id, check_in_url, appliance_credential, last_checkin_at to license_state
The 'Connect this appliance' claim-code control redeems a code
Redeem calls C4 /register
Register response (credential, check_in_url, first JWT) is stored in license_state
Appliance credential is stored hashed, not plaintext, in license_state
license_token is set to the first JWT after a successful connect
license_state remains a single row after connect (singleton invariant)
The refresh job calls C4 /check-in on schedule
Refresh overwrites license_token when a new JWT is returned
A freshly refreshed token takes effect (no stale verify cache)
Refresh no-ops on a 'no change' result
Refresh leaves the current token on a revoked result (grace)
After exp passes with no refresh, resolveSelfHostTier drops to essentials
Worst-case disconnection still yields >=30d grace (exp = grace + poll window)
License page shows connected status + last check-in time
Entering a new claim code rebinds (replaces the stored credential)
Air-gap paste still works alongside connect
Invalid/expired/consumed claim code at connect shows a clear error
Connected vs air-gap state is shown distinctly on the License page
getClientContracts returns the client's license contracts
The Licenses tab/route renders at a route distinct from the account stub
The list shows tier/term/expiry/renewal status
The 'Download key' action links to the stored document
Key download is served via the existing portal Documents mechanism
Empty state shows when the client has no license contracts
A portal nav entry for Licenses is present
A portal user sees only their own client's license contracts
A portal user cannot download another client's key document (authz)
Existing client-portal Documents page still works (regression)
Shared C4 contract types round-trip between alga-psa and the service shapes
The C4 client module calls /sign with the service credential
The C4 client module surfaces structured errors to callers
Missing C4 base URL / credential produces a clear startup error
No ES256 private key / PEM is present anywhere in the alga-psa repo
The Temporal worker holds no signing key and only calls /sign
Documentation lists the appliance SKUs and the C4 API contract
E2E connected happy path: order -> pay -> issue -> connect -> refresh
E2E air-gap happy path: order -> pay -> issue -> paste -> licensed
E2E idempotency: a duplicated webhook yields exactly one license/contract/document
License contract status auto-expires off end_date (contract behavior)
A second appliance registration under the same subscription rebinds/replaces (single-appliance v1)
The C4 container image builds and starts, serving /healthz (scaffold + Dockerfile/k8s)
Issuance records the C4-side entitlement keyed to the Stripe subscription (current token retrievable on check-in)
